🚪 Door Schedule

{{ device.device_name }} — {{ device.serial_number }}

🕐 App local time: — Scheduler status: {% if sched_status and sched_status.running %} ● running {% else %} ● stopped {% endif %} {% if sched_status and sched_status.last_check %} (last check: {{ sched_status.last_check }}) {% endif %}
{% if table_missing %}
Setup required: The door_schedules table does not exist yet in Supabase. Run the migration SQL in your Supabase SQL Editor before using this feature. See docs/door_schedules_migration.sql.
{% else %} {# ── How it works ── #}

⏰ App-Managed Schedule

The SmartPetHome backend checks this schedule every 10 seconds and sends open_door / close_door MQTT commands at the configured times. The ESP32 does not manage time internally.

  • Each action fires once per day at the specified time.
  • Leave a time blank to skip that action.
  • Disable the schedule to pause both actions without deleting it.
  • Each execution creates an alert and Telegram notification.
{# ── Schedule form ── #}

🗓️ {{ 'Edit Schedule' if door_sched else 'Create Schedule' }}

Leave blank to skip automatic open.
Leave blank to skip automatic close.
{# ── Current schedule summary ── #} {# ── Test buttons ── #}

🧪 Test Scheduled Actions

Send a command immediately — same as the scheduler would send at the scheduled time. MQTT must be connected and the device must be online.

{% if door_sched %}

📋 Current Schedule

Open at
{% if door_sched.open_time %} {{ door_sched.open_time[:5] }} {% else %} — not set {% endif %}
Close at
{% if door_sched.close_time %} {{ door_sched.close_time[:5] }} {% else %} — not set {% endif %}
Status
{{ 'Enabled' if door_sched.enabled else 'Disabled' }}
{% endif %} {% endif %}{# end table_missing #} {% endblock %}
